sevenDayForecast.headline: High risk of a tropical cyclone developing near the northeast Queensland coast later this evening.
points: Forecast Track Maps and Tropical Cyclone Advices are being issued for Tropical Low 12U and should be referred to for more information. A Severe Weather Warning and Flood Watches and Warnings are also current.
A deepening monsoon low (12U) lies to the south southwest of Willis Island and is moving south southwest, towards the northeast Queensland coast.
The low is forecast to develop slightly as it moves closer to the coast, with the risk of development into a tropical cyclone being High from later this evening through to Sunday morning.
The system is expected to cross the coast early Sunday morning, moving inland and weakening below a tropical cyclone during Sunday afternoon.
In the longer term there is a risk that a weakened 12U moves to the northwest, over Gulf of Carpentaria waters. If this occurs there is a Low risk of developing into a tropical cyclone from Friday.

trackForecast.headline: Tropical Low 12U developing northwest of Willis Island, impacts likely during today.
trackForecast.discussion:
A tropical low (12U) is developing in the Coral Sea, northwest of Willis Island. It is moving south southwest towards the North Tropical Coast and likely to cross the coast between Innisfail and Bowen early Sunday.

Tropical Low 12U is rated a High probability (60%) of developing into a tropical cyclone prior to landfall. However, the heavy rainfall and damaging wind impacts are likely to be similar whether or not it becomes a tropical cyclone.

The Abbott Point area is currently under a Tropical Cyclone Warning. Gales with damaging wind gusts to 100 km/h are possible about the area this afternoon, and are more likely if the system tracks slightly further east than forecast. Heavy to intense rainfall is also possible in the area today and on Sunday.

After crossing the coast, the winds of the system will quickly weaken and the risk of gales should rapidly ease. Heavy rain may continue, however.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound is based on the system developing more rapidly than forecast in a more favourable environment, and being slower to reach the coast thus having more time to develop.
